type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Oxides of nitrogen increased from 144.409 in 2004 to 165.90 in 2005 as production volumes increased resulting in more fuel requirements to operate the boilers.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: Larger volume of instrumentation equipment containing mercury was taken out of service and the mercury send out for recycling. Mercury to recycling was 13.09 kg. in 2004 and increased to 17.01 kg. in 2005.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Oxides of nitrogen increased from 116.586 in 2003 to 144.409 in 2004 as production volumes increased resulting in more fuel increased fuel requirements to operate the boilers.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increase was 10.41%.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Production volumes increased resulting in increased fuel requirements to operate the boilers. Majority of sulphur dioxide emissions are from using Bunker #6 fuel oil which complemented the use of natural gas. Sulphur dioxide emissions increased from 290.5

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The 13.09 kg. of mercury which were removed from facility were send out for treatment and recycling.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: Program initiated to recycle waste flourescent lamps. In addition the disposal of obsolete mercury containing instrumentation such as Mercoid switches, Manometers, thermometers, etc. is now reported as send out for recycling.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Mercury emissions through stacks were increased from .172 kg. to .233 kg. as the production volume were increased and the volume of bunker #6 fuel oil was increased as a fuel source.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Production volumes increased in 2004 which required increased volumes of fuel to operate the boilers.  Majority of increase was from using Bunker #6 oil as one of the sources of fuel to complement the use of natural gas.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Production volumes increased resulting in increased fuel requirements to operate the boilers. Majority of release was from using Bunker #6 oil as one of the sources of fuel complementing the use of natural gas. Amount of PM10 emitted increased from 17.802

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Production levels increased requiring increased fuel volumes to operate boilers. Majority of PM2.5 emissions are from using Bunker #6 fuel oil which was used to complement natural gas.  PM 2.5 emissions increased from 11.767 tonnes in 2003 to 18.005 tonne

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Oxides of nitrogen decreased from 138.252 tonnes in 2002 to 116.586 tonnes in 2003 as production levels decreased resulting in less fuel required to operate boiler systems.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Decrease from 49.58 tonnes in 2002 to 35.82 tonnes in 2003 is due to a reduction in production operations.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Sulphur Dioxide emissions increased from 54.89 tonnes in 2002 to 290.570 tonnes in 2003 as the proportion of Bunker # 6 fuel oil was increased in complementing natural gas as the fuel sources for operating the boilers.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: There was a slight increase in the number of measuring devices which contained mercury and were disposed of as hazardous wastes. eg. mercury thermometers, Mercoid control switched and Manometers.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Mercury emissions through stacks were increased from .138 kg. in 2002 to .172kg in 2003 as the volume of bunker #6 fuel oil was increased as a fuel source.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The amount of PM10 emitted increased from 6.889 tonnes in 2002 to 19.332 tonnes in 2003 as the proportion of Bunker # 6 fuel oil  used was increased in complementing natural gas as the fuels required to operate boilers.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The PM2.5 emissions increased from 5.723 tonnes in 2002 to 13.27 tonnes in 2003 as the proportion of Bunker # 6 fuel oil was increased in complementing natural gas as the fuels required to operate boilers.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination
